summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that TableChatAgent with full_eval=True executes arbitrary Python code via the eval() function in the pandas_eval tool handler. The exploit creates a file in the container filesystem, proving code execution capability.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the malicious pandas expression to execute arbitrary Python code that creates /tmp/pwned.txt in the container, demonstrating full code execution capabilities when full_eval=True is enabled.
actual_observed_result: The exploit script successfully executed the malicious payload __import__('os').system('touch /tmp/pwned.txt') through the pandas_eval tool handler, creating the proof file in the container filesystem.
verification_method: The run script checks for the existence of /tmp/pwned.txt after executing the exploit. The file presence confirms that arbitrary code execution occurred via the eval() sink when full_eval=True bypassed the sanitize_command() protection.
evidence_produced_by_run: The eval() function at table_chat_agent.py:239 was called with malicious code
The full_eval=True configuration bypassed the sanitize_command() protection
Arbitrary code execution was achieved through the pandas expression evaluation mechanism
The proof file /tmp/pwned.txt was created, confirming successful exploitation

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts it into the container at /target-repo.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: main branch (vulnerable at time of audit)
